title: Hit by an Uninsured Driver in San Antonio, Texas (2026)

The good news: Texas law and your own auto policy may provide more protection than you think. Uninsured and underinsured motorist (UM/UIM) coverage, personal injury claims, and direct legal action against at-fault drivers are all paths worth exploring — but navigating them alone is risky.

The most important step you can take right now is talking to a local attorney who knows Texas law and the San Antonio court system. The right legal guidance can make a huge difference in what you're able to recover — for medical bills, lost wages, and pain and suffering.

reddit_title: Hit by an uninsured driver in San Antonio, TX — what are your actual options for recovering damages?
reddit_body:
If you've been hit by an uninsured driver in San Antonio, the first thing to know is that you're not automatically out of luck. Texas doesn't require uninsured motorist coverage, but if you opted into it on your own policy, you can file a UM/UIM claim directly with your insurer to cover medical bills, lost wages, and other damages — even though the other driver had no coverage.

If you don't have UM/UIM coverage, you still have options. You can pursue a personal injury lawsuit directly against the at-fault driver. It's harder to collect if they have no assets, but an attorney can help evaluate whether it's worth pursuing and identify other liable parties you might not have considered — like a vehicle owner or employer if the driver was on the job.

The key is acting quickly. Texas has a two-year statute of limitations on personal injury claims, and evidence disappears fast. A local San Antonio attorney can review your case for free and lay out your realistic options. If you need help finding one, wreckmatch.com matches accident victims with experienced local attorneys at no cost.
